feed.title: SAP Community - Document Information Extraction
feed.subtitle: Document Information Extraction Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Unable to create SAP Document AI Trial instance (plan blocks_of_100)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are currently testing the SAP Document AI Trial service and have encountered an issue when trying to create a new instance — either via Booster or manually through the BTP Cockpit.

Each attempt fails with the following message:
Couldn't create an instance of service 'SAP Document AI Trial' with plan 'blocks_of_100'.
Operation failed.

The same behavior occurs across multiple trial subaccounts and for different users. Additionally, previously created instances cannot be deleted and show the status “Deletion Failed”.

We understand this is a trial environment, but could you please advise whether this behavior is expected or if there is any known limitation or temporary issue affecting the SAP Document AI Trial service?

Any guidance or confirmation would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your help and time.

entry.title: Tutorial Use Machine Learning to Extract Information from Documents with Swagger UI - error 500
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm following the tutorial Use Machine Learning to Extract Information from Documents with Swagger UI | SAP Tutorials.

When I try querying or posting data, it always returns error 500.

I'm using Trial subaccount - service instance SAP Document AI Trial - plan Default.

Has anyone encountered this issue?

Regards,

Tri

entry.title: Best Practices for Training Custom Invoice Templates in SAP Document AI (Base Version)
entry.content:
📘 Summary:

Looking for best practices on how to handle schema updates and reuse trained templates in SAP Document Information Extraction (DOX) Base Version. After adding new fields through schema versioning, previous training and mappings are not retained. Need guidance on whether retraining is required or if there’s a migration/reuse option.

Scenario:

I’ve been working on a custom invoice extraction setup using SAP Document Information Extraction (DOX) – Base Version (non-generative).
The use case involves 500 invoices with different layouts.

I created:

  • One custom schema

  • Around 100 templates, each trained manually with “Setup Type = Manual” for all fields

  • Training done successfully for ~100 invoices

 New Requirement:

Later, we received a request to add 5 new fields to the existing schema.
However, DOX doesn’t allow adding fields to an active schema version that already has templates.
To add new fields, we must:

  1. Deactivate and delete the existing templates, or

  2. Create a new schema version

I proceeded with creating a new schema version and added the new fields.

 Challenge:

When uploading invoices using the new schema version, the existing training/mappings from version 1 are not available.
It behaves as a completely new schema — none of the previously trained fields or templates are reused.

 Question / Clarification Needed:

Do I need to retrain all 500 invoices again for the new schema version, or
Is there any recommended approach or migration method to reuse the field mappings from the previous version?

🚀 Discussion Points / Best Practices (to confirm with SAP team):

  • Is there any way to migrate or copy field mappings between schema versions?

  • What’s the best practice when new fields need to be added to a schema with multiple templates?

  • Should we plan all required fields before starting training to avoid versioning issues?

  • Does SAP DOX Premium (Generative AI) handle such cases more flexibly?

entry.title: Hello, what about SAP IA JOULE?
entry.content:
Can a sap consultant get the SAP AI Joule?

BR

entry.title: Outlook about SAP Document AI - Channels and workflow features
entry.content:
Hi Document AI experts

According to the latest update of Document AI documentation, "The integration of the Channels and Workflows features lets you set up inbound channels that route incoming emails to appropriately configured workflows, automating sophisticated document-processing tasks."

I have a request to make an automatic load of PDFs attached to emails in a outlook email folder and I was thinking about making a solution utilizing the MS-Graph API. Now it seems that SAP Document AI has build in functionality to handle this request, so I have put my MS-Graph idea on hold and hope that someone in here can share some experience and maybe even a guide how to set this up.

Kind Regards
Thomas Madsen Nielsen

entry.title: SAP AI for SAP Transportation management
entry.content:
Hello,

Iam looking for an SAP AI just for the purpose of finding relevant information on SAP Transportation management relating to any issue or concept. That means i want to give a prompt and expects SAP AI to serach for information across sap ecosystem i.e. SAP Notes etc.

I have access to sap learning hub and practice system in the hub. Is it possible to use SAP AI in hub for the above question and if not how can i get a trial system of SAP AI for the above objective ?

rgds

S

entry.title: Unexpected Line Item Extraction Behavior in Purchase Orders using SAP Document AI
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We are currently using SAP Document Information Extraction (Premium Edition) and we have created a custom schema based on SAP’s standard Purchase Order schema.

The issue we are facing is related to line item extraction.
When we submit multi-page Purchase Orders, DOX often extracts line items from tables that are NOT the correct PO item table, and in many cases, those tables are located on different pages of the document. These “wrong” tables have a very similar structure to the real item table, which seems to confuse the detection.

What we have tried so far

1️⃣Edited documents in the DOX UI, corrected the fields, confirmed the results and submitted feedback (ground truth).
2️⃣Created a Template, associated the corrected documents to it, and executed extraction again using this template.

However, even after multiple training attempts, DOX still extracts line items from the wrong table/page in many cases.

Constraints

Splitting the PDF is not an option, because the “wrong” table is not always on the same page (for example, it is not always page 2). The page position is not consistent.

My questions

  • Shouldn’t this be resolved by using templates and providing repeated feedback?

  • Is this a limitation of DOX template training?

  • Is there any recommended approach or best practice to handle scenarios where multiple similar tables exist across different pages?

Any guidance, clarification, or alternative approach would be really appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Question bout how to install doc AI and chose editon and version and question about pricing
entry.content:
Hi

I hope the community can help me clarify two Document AI questions.

1) First question is about installation and how to choose edition and version

The documentation is talking a lot about functionality limited to the premium edition, but I have found absolutely no documentation about how to get a premium edition installed.

Basically all I can find about installation is below page. It says noting about base edition vs Premium edition and it says noting about how to install v1 or v2. I assume that I have to choose edition and version?

As far as I can see the boosters are only for Free Tier, so also no help to get there.

2) My second question is about the pricing

I think the pricing is cryptic. Again the pricing is related to the edition and version. From the anti-overview in the documentation I see that base edition is only for version 1 and that premium edition is available in both flavours V1 and V2. So far so good although that primarily brings me back to the first question - how to choose edition and version during installation (Enabling).

I can also see that base edition and premium edition are both metered on blocks of 100 documents. The base editon is additionally metered on "compute hours" where the premium edition is metered on requests and users.

(Here I notice that Premium Edition/Version 1 does not have metering of compute hours and also no metering of "Requsts and Users" - only blocks of documents. I wonder if this is just an error in the documentation?)

So far so complicated and still no idea what the cost is.

The documentation also mention that a block of 100 documents cost about 20 EUR - sometimes a document counts for more than one document, but so far ok understandable and it seems to be a ok price.

1 Compute hour cost 1 EUR - yeehaa that's cheap
1 template activation cost 5 compute hours - ok, then forget about the "yeehaa that's cheap"
1 transaction(document) cost 1 compute second - yeehaa that's very cheep
1 compute hour = 500 compute seconds - wait is an hour not 3600 seconds  - ok, then forget about the "yeehaa that's very cheap"

Then in the middle of the metering and pricing info is a insert stating that by HR the pricing is different :

Back on track with the pricing for the premium edition.

With premium version 1 and 2 now the minimum block size is 500 documents a month and the minimum price is 300 EUR, so this raise the price per document from 20 cent to 60 cent  with a little discount if you load extremely many documents.

 

Finally the premium edition version 2 has an additional metering/pricing for " Requests and Users". If I try to add this to the pricing formular, my head begins to spin, I forget all I just learned and I have to start all over again.

Currently I have no idea how to choose edition and version and I have no idea what the cost would be if I managed to choose a edition and version. The boosters only help to install a free edition, but that is not really what we need.

Best Regards
Thomas Madsen Nielsen

entry.title: Question about best practices for clients in SAP document AI?
entry.content:
Hi all

The word CLIENT seems very familiar when you're used to work with SAP ECC or S/4 HANA, but in BTP we have other options to separate data and maybe we would create subaccounts for the same purpose as clients in the ERP system.

So I wonder what we can use clients in SAP Document AI for.

Lets pretend that I have 5 different SAP Document AI projects 

  • load customer PO to sale sorder UK
  • Load customer PO to sales order PL
  • Load vendor invoice 
  • Load WM inventory documents
  • Load MM inventory documents

Now, I would very much like to separate schemas and loaded documents etc. for these project so they are not just in the same big pile of documents. Could I make this separation by creating one client per project?

Best regards
Thomas Madsen Nielsen

entry.title: Can SAP DOCUMENT AI make automatic template selection?
entry.content:
This question was actually also asked back in 2022, but SAP Document AI has been updated 50-100 times since, so maybe a solution exist today.
We use Document AI basic edition.
We use SAP_purchaseOrder_schema or a extended copy of the same. We load purchase orders from different customers with different layouts and we see a lot of problems with the OCR not working in a consistent way. We hope we can fix some of these problems with templates. We would probably need templates for most of our customers POs.
We don't have a secure way to automatically determine which template to use before we load the PDFs.
Currently we can make a manual process where a user must open the pdf to see which customer it comes from and then manually load the pdf with the correct schema and template. This manual load process does not fulfill our goal for an automatic process.

The best solution will of course be if the OCR could be better so we wouldn't need a template, but a solution from the drawer with second best options could be if SAP Document AI automatic could detected the correct template from the templates related to the chosen schema.

Here is a a couple of examples of problems we hope to solve with templates. To be honest I don't think SAP Document AI performs very well in these cases:

1) A customer has more lines in the PDF for each item. The second line for an item has a scheduling line item no and SAP document AI reads this as a new item instead of just information for the previous item:

2) With line items that has identical layouts SAP Document AI suddenly swich from identifying a "Supplier material no" to instead identify a "Customer material no":

3) On page 1 SAP Document AI does not identify the "Supplier material no", but on page 2 it does.

Page 1: 

Page 2:

 

Best regards
Thomas Madsen Nielsen

entry.title: How to manage documents in SAP Document AI
entry.content:
Hi, 

The tutorial https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cp-aibus-dox-swagger-ui.html shows how to upload documents in SAP Document AI via API call. So I have following questions. 

1. If I develop a solution e.g. in SAP Build Process Automation to call SAP Document API, then there will numerous documents that will get uploaded in the document AI to extract information. After an extraction is over, the document is not used again.

Because I think we can't reuse the same document already uploaded instead of user again uploading it. So is there any best practice on how to manage the huge volume of documents after extraction is over. Should we set up another automation to delete the historical documents via calling an API?

2. In this tutorial, we are first creating a client. Is this an one time activity? Is this done to keep my test separate from others. In the productive use case, do I really need to setup client. The Document AI UI doesn't provide the option to client management.(at lease the base plan).

Thanks,
Mainak

entry.title: DOX: Fields now identified that does not exist in schema
entry.content:
After loading a file with the SAP_purchaseOrder_schema, a lot of new fields that does not exist in the Schema is identified. I haven't checked all the fields, but it seems that there is now a lot of fieldnames starting with "Buyer....",  but these fields doesn't exist in the schema. Example below shows the field Buyer ID was found, but the schema doesn't have a field Buyer ID.

So what is going on here?

 

Her is all the schema fields - none starting with Buyer....





Best regards
Thomas Madsen Nielsen

entry.title: SAP Document AI – Quality Certificate processed successfully but Certificate Receipt remains in “Exp
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

 

I am working on the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition scenario
“AI-assisted processing of incoming supplier quality certificates with SAP Document AI”.

 

Current setup:
- AI Units are procured
- Business catalog QM – Incoming Quality Certificates (SAP_QM_BC_INCG_QLTY_CERT_PC) is assigned
- Scope item 7TC (Integration with SAP Document AI) is activated
- SAP Document AI tenant is accessible
- Certificate Receipt is created automatically at Goods Receipt with status = Expected

 

What I have done:
1. Posted Goods Receipt – Certificate Receipt created in Manage Certificate Receipts (F3233) with status Expected
2. Uploaded the supplier quality certificate PDF in SAP Document AI using:
   - Document Type: Custom
   - Schema: SAP_OCROnly_schema
3. Document AI processing completes successfully and shows status = Done

 

Issue:
Even after successful processing in SAP Document AI, the Certificate Receipt in SAP S/4HANA Cloud remains in “Expected” status and is not automatically updated to “Received”.
The document is not automatically attached to the Certificate Receipt.

 

Questions:
1. Is automatic update of Certificate Receipt (Expected → Received) the expected behavior in this scenario?
2. Is manual confirmation in Manage Certificate Receipts (F3233) the standard SAP-supported fallback if automatic matching does not occur?
3. Does the automatic update require uploading the document specifically via the app “Upload Quality Certificate Documents (F8858)” instead of uploading through the SAP Document AI portal?
4. If the “Upload Quality Certificate Documents (F8858)” app is used and it is not available in the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library, how can access to this app be obtained in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
5.  Are there any additional configuration or prerequisites required to enable automatic linkage between SAP Document AI and Certificate Receipts?

 

Looking for confirmation from experts who have implemented or tested this scenario.

 

Thank you.

entry.title: globalTradeItemNumber in the standard document type Invoice for extraction with SAP Document AI
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

How can the GlobalTradeItemNumber be implemented or taken into account in the standard document type “invoice” so that it can be extracted from invoices using SAP Document AI?

Best regards

SAP Document AI SAP Ariba Invoicing

entry.title: "Destination is not a valid DGS destination" - Joule for Consultants Console custom documents setup
entry.content:
Hello Community,

 

I am trying to set up SharePoint document grounding in the Joule for Consultants Console (custom-documents section) - section user specific knowledge grounding. After connecting to my destinations-service instande all destinations show "Destination is not a valid DGS destination" regardless of configuration. I have tried OAuth2ClientCredentials and OAuth2Password, both with graph.microsoft.com as URL. The grounding-rag resource group has ext.ai.sap.com/document-grounding=true. An existing grounding pipeline with the same SharePoint destination works successfully. Even the auto-generated destinations show the same error. What exactly does the DGS validator check, and what is the correct destination configuration for this specific console?

 

Best Regards

entry.title: Destination is not a valid DGS destination - Joule for Consultants Console custom documents setup
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I am trying to set up SharePoint document grounding in the Joule for Consultants Console (specific knowledge-grounding section). All destinations show "Destination is not a valid DGS destination" regardless of configuration. I have tried OAuth2ClientCredentials and OAuth2Password, both with graph.microsoft.com as URL. The grounding-rag resource group has ext.ai.sap.com/document-grounding=true. An existing grounding pipeline with the same SharePoint destination works successfully. Even the auto-generated  destinations show the same error. What exactly does the DGS validator check, and what is the correct destination configuration for this specific console?

 

Best Regards

entry.title: What is your opinion on Document AI - line item capabilty?
entry.content:
I wonder what experience other people have with Document AI.

To be honest I'm not impressed and especially the reading of line items seems to be poor. So I'm very interested in knowing what's others experience with this?

In below example it repeats 3 times the same line item 2. I can see why it get confused, but it should be a simple check that it should not output 3 items with the same data.

entry.title: How to handle a schema change in SAP Document AI
entry.content:
Hi

We have build a process on SAP standard schema SAP_purchaseOrder_schema and we have made templates for different customers and to each template is attached a few documents.

Now a new requirement to extract a special danish number called an EAN nummer. In this context an EAN number is a "digital customer address" for use in EDI when we send EDI invoices. Usually we use the Tax ID to identify the customer, but some customers does not include the tax ID in their purchase order PDF. Instead they include the EAN number which can also be used to identify the customer.
TO include the EAN field i need to make a modified customer specific copy of the standard schema.

That was the background and now to the question. When I make a new schema, can I then reassign the templates including the attached documents to this new schema or do I have to build new templates?

As the existing templates does not know the new field, I guess I will have to start all over and create new templates and that lead me to next question. Will this schema change influence on existing training? (If existing training exist - I don't understand how this training works in combination with templates - no, I don't understand how it works at all).

Best Regards
Thomas

entry.title: Can we anywhere see when SAP change the rules in SAP Docuent AI?
entry.content:
Can we see if and when SAP changed the LLM or other rules in the OCR part of SAP Document AI.

We use SAP Document AI via API calls and today we have had a number of requests that timed out. When we look in the UI, the documents are "DONE", but the API call did not answer within 1 minute. The response is usually much faster and we have never seen this problem before. Also when we re-load the documents the response time is about 10-20 seconds. 

The reason I suspect some updates is that after some time, the timeout stopped, but now the data is suddenly OCR extracted in an other way. Before we had an line item OCR error - we had same error since we starte the project a few months ago. Now this error  is solved and instead we see a new error.
Best Regards

Thomas Madsen Nielsen

entry.title: SAP's agentic moment is here but a few practical questions I keep hearing
entry.content:
Watching the Autonomous Enterprise vision come together since Sapphire has been genuinely exciting. Joule Studio hitting GA, the A2A protocol opening up, Claude in the reasoning layer, SAP has done something important: it's made "agents that execute, not just assist" the default expectation for ERP. That's a rising tide for everyone working in this space.

(Quick disclosure: I'm the founder of agentnomics.ai, where we build a vendor-neutral agent layer for SAP, so I'm in this every day.)

What I find interesting is the set of practical questions this surge is surfacing for smaller and mid-market SAP shops, the teams I spend most of my time with:

  • Cost predictability. Consumption-based AI Units are flexible, but a lot of finance leaders are finding the annual number hard to forecast, especially with overage rates and expiring credits. Budgeting for agents shouldn't require an ITAM owner watching SAP for me every month.
  • Estates that aren't clean cloud S/4HANA yet. Joule is strongest on the newest landscapes. Plenty of healthy businesses are still on ECC or heavily customized hybrid setups and want agentic value now, via OData/RFC, without a full migration first.
  • Life beyond SAP. Most teams don't live only in SAP they're also in Salesforce, ServiceNow, and commerce tools like Shopify and Square. The dream is one agent across all of it.

The approach we've taken is to meet teams where they are: flat-rate so the cost is the cost, model-agnostic so you're not married to one LLM's pricing, secure governed write-back across SAP and non-SAP systems, and live in weeks rather than quarters. It's complementary to where SAP is heading, just aimed squarely at the cost-conscious mid-market.

Genuinely curious how others here are thinking about it: if you're rolling out agents on ECC or hybrid, how are you handling cost forecasting and cross-system reach?
